summary: Second gm_1601 decompilation pass that substantially advanced matching for the game-mode/result-related translation unit. The PR replaced many nonmatching placeholders and weak prototypes with typed implementations/signatures, completed gm_1601 data/bss/sdata matching according to the decomp.dev report, refined MatchEnd/MatchPlayerData/StartMeleeRules-related layout, and adjusted GALE01 symbols/splits for corrected SJIS/string sizes and section boundaries. Evidence is strongest for matching progress and struct/header cleanup; behavioral intent beyond names and data access patterns is mostly inferred from code shape.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; added/rewrote implementations and matching-oriented rewrites for many gm_1601 functions.
Updated public prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or loose int/void* forms to bool/u8/u16/MatchEnd and more precise signatures.
Refined static struct layout and static storage declarations, including lbl_8046B378, lbl_8046B488_t fields, and lbl_8046B668 as a single struct rather than an array macro.
Refined game-mode result data structures, especially MatchTeamData, MatchPlayerData, MatchEnd, and gmm_x1868 field typing, with STATIC_ASSERT size checks.
Adjusted lbl_8046B668_t and StartMeleeRules::x58, and added PerfLabelLine/lbl_8046B378_t structs with size asserts.
Narrowed accessor return types for gmMainLib_8015CCF0 and gmMainLib_8015CDB0.
Mirrored typed accessor prototypes used by gm_1601 and soundtest.
Corrected many gm_1601 data/sdata SJIS and string object sizes/scopes plus sbss size.
Updated gm_1601 and gm_16F1 rodata/sdata boundaries after data matching.
Adjusted calls to gm_80164330 after its return type became bool, using explicit u8 casts to preserve matching.
Updated local pointer type for gmMainLib_8015CCF0 from int* to u32*.
Used the newly identified MatchPlayerData field x34 instead of pad_x30 indexing.

what_changed: Added or substantially filled in gm_1601 implementations including gm_80160C90, gm_8016247C, gm_80162800, gm_80162A98, gm_80163374, gm_801634D4, gm_80163B9C, gm_80164330, gm_8016468C, gm_80164F18, fn_801652D8, fn_80165D60, gm_80166A98, gm_80168638, fn_80169900, fn_8016A09C, and gm_8016A22C as shown by removed placeholder markers and bot match report.

Improved existing near-matches in gm_1601 with matching-sensitive rewrites, including temporary aliases in gm_80160B40, explicit tmp variables in gm_8016279C, and unsigned saturation expressions around comparisons to (u32)-1.

Converted many gm_1601 prototypes from unknown or overly broad types to more precise signatures: examples include gm_80160C90 taking bool, gm_801634D4 returning bool with u8* arguments, gm_80164330 returning bool, fn_80165D60 taking/returning MatchEnd*, and gm_80166A98 taking a detailed MatchEnd/result parameter list.

Refined result/match structs in gm/types.h: MatchTeamData received offset comments and a 0xC STATIC_ASSERT; MatchPlayerData exposed x30, x34, and padding with a 0xA8 assert; MatchEnd received full offset comments and a 0x227C assert.
Changed gmm_x1868::x1A48 from s32 to u32 and changed gmMainLib_8015CCF0 to return u32*, aligning accessor type with unsigned cumulative counters used in gm_1601 and soundtest.
Changed gmMainLib_8015CDB0 to return s32* and propagated that typing into gm_1601.

Refined gm_1601 static data declarations: added static lbl_8046B378, expanded lbl_8046B488_t fields, changed lbl_8046B668 from an array keyed by NUM_LBL_8046B668 to a single struct, and removed NUM_LBL_8046B668 from forward.h.
Added PerfLabelLine and lbl_8046B378_t definitions in mn/types.h, and changed StartMeleeRules::x58 from pointer-to-array-of-27 to struct lbl_8046B668_t*.
Updated mnstagesel call sites to cast gm_80164330 result to u8, likely to preserve codegen after the function was typed as bool.

Corrected many config/GALE01/symbols.txt SJIS/string sizes and local scopes around lbl_803D4C48 through lbl_803D4F10 and lbl_804D3EF8 through lbl_804D3F88, and adjusted lbl_804D659A size from 0x6 to 0x2.

Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt rodata/sdata boundaries: gm_1601 rodata end changed from 0x803B7A60 to 0x803B7A44, gm_1601 sdata end from 0x804D3F68 to 0x804D3F67, and gm_16F1 rodata start shifted accordingly.

The decomp.dev report recorded 25 new matches, including gm_1601 .data, .bss, .sdata, .rodata, gm_801634D4, gm_80163374, gm_8016468C, gm_80164F18, fn_80165D60, gm_80168638, fn_8016A09C, and gm_80162800.
smart_moves: Used precise struct layout work plus STATIC_ASSERTs to lock in offsets and sizes before relying on field access in matching code.
Typed gmmain_lib accessors at the source instead of repeatedly casting at every call site, reducing ambiguity and improving downstream decompilation.
Preserved codegen after semantic cleanup by adding explicit casts where necessary, such as casting gm_80164330's bool result to u8 in mnstagesel.
Separated real data-size corrections from code changes by updating symbols.txt and splits.txt alongside the C changes, allowing data sections to reach 100% matching.
Replaced padding-index access with named fields once layout evidence existed, shown by gm_17AD moving from player_standings[arg0].pad_x30[4] to player_standings[arg0].x34.
Kept matching-sensitive unsigned saturation idioms explicit, e.g. comparisons against (u32)-1 and MAX/MIN-like forms for cumulative counters.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling gm result/state code, invest early in MatchEnd and MatchPlayerData offsets; a single field discovery can clean up multiple callers outside the target file.
Changing a function's return type to bool can alter caller codegen; callers may require explicit u8 casts if the original ABI/codegen treated the value as an 8-bit quantity.
Data matching for text-heavy modules may require correcting SJIS string object sizes and scopes rather than only changing C declarations.

Pointer-to-array versus pointer-to-struct distinctions matter for matching and API clarity; this PR changed StartMeleeRules::x58 and gm_8016A98C away from an array-of-27 model after evidence indicated a single lbl_8046B668_t object.
For cumulative counters, signedness of fields/accessors is not cosmetic: changing gmMainLib_8015CCF0 and gmm_x1868::x1A48 to u32 aligned with saturation logic and other users.
Use STATIC_ASSERTs when replacing padding with named fields, especially for result structs with unusual packed offsets such as MatchEnd team_standings at 0x1B and player_standings at 0x58.
naming_conventions: Kept address-derived names for unknown functions and data such as gm_8016247C, fn_80165D60, lbl_8046B488_t, lbl_8046B668_t, and lbl_803D4C48 when semantic names were not proven.
Used x-offset field names for newly exposed but semantically unknown struct members, e.g. x30, x34, x1A48, x1AC, x1AD, x1B2.
Used descriptive names only where meaning was already evident or conventional, such as MatchEnd, MatchPlayerData, MatchTeamData, StartMeleeRules, PerfLabelLine, and player_standings.
Retained fn_ prefix for local/static or less semantically identified functions and gm_ prefix for game-mode functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced local temporaries solely for codegen, such as tmp_text/tmp_ckind in gm_80160B40 and tmp in gm_8016279C.
Used explicit unsigned casts and comparisons to (u32)-1 for saturation behavior, including patterns like `(var_r4 > (u32)-1) ? (u32)-1 : var_r4`.
Used explicit casts at call sites after type refinement to avoid breaking matching, notably `(u8) gm_80164330(...)` in mnstagesel.
Adjusted function signatures and accessors to avoid repeated void* casts and produce cleaner, more matchable field accesses.
Corrected section boundaries in splits.txt after rodata/sdata size discoveries, preventing adjacent TU data from being attributed to the wrong unit.
Corrected symbol sizes/scopes for SJIS strings to make gm_1601 .data, .sdata, .rodata, and .bss report as fully matched.

review_feedback.text: Author opened the PR for discussion and noted it was initially not ready/needed for review.

Reported matched code 53.47% (+0.08%, +2992 bytes), matched data 20.93% (+0.38%, +4555 bytes), and 25 new matches. It also reported two broken matches: mnStageSel_802599EC and gm_8016A164, plus one gm_16F1 .rodata regression.
Approved with: "Nice work, thanks." No inline review comments were present in the dump.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fix the reported gm_8016A164 near-match or mnStageSel_802599EC regression from PR #2185.
Search for subsequent semantic renames of lbl_8046B488_t, lbl_8046B668_t, and lbl_8046B378_t.
Search later gm_1601 work for completion of partially improved functions such as gm_80166A98, fn_80169900, gm_8016A22C, gm_80163B9C, and gm_80164330.
Compare PR #2185 with gm_1601 part 1 to understand the struct-layout progression for MatchEnd and StartMeleeRules.
